prompt:
Should schools pay much attention to prepare students for exams? Many people are arguing about this topic nowadays. In my opinion, the best idea is to try to find a balance between preparing for exams and educating other school subjects.

Let's start with the importance of preparing for exams.  I believe, that in highschool it is the main thing, that greatly determines to the child's future. For example, in Russia students write state exam after school. If you write it well, you would be able to study in a great university in a big city. If you don't - then you would have to study in a weaker regional university. Obviosly, to pass all the exams, you should prepare for them. And prepare well.

From the other hand, graduates, who are aimed only to the preparing for exams, usually spend all their efforts to the main subjects. That leads to the awful situation: students don't know at least anything in other things. It is also bad, because students in school should improve in different fields.

Summing up, I believe, that in highschool it is really important to pay attention to preparing for exams. But at the same time schools shouldn't forget about other subjects.

If you don't - then you would have to study in a weaker regional university. Obviosly, to pass all the exams, you should prepare for them. And prepare well.

From the other hand, graduates, who are aimed only to the preparing for exams, usually spend all their efforts to the main subjects. That leads to the awful situation: students don't know at least anything in other things. It is also bad, because students in school should improve in different fields.

CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07010","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hey there! I've already finished the first semester, so I can help you. Here are some tips to have more free time:

	* Do tasks when they published, not at the last day. For example you thought that the homework will take one day, and after some time you realize that it is harder and you need to spend more time on it. But because you started earlier you have a lot of days before deadline, so you can do this homework easily.
	* Make plan and stick to it. You should know when you need to do certain homework, so you won't spend time on choosing task.
	* Don't forget to chill sometimes. You need to have at least one hour free time per day.
	* Sleep 7+ hours. If you think, that you can get more time by sleeping less -- you are wrong. You'll be exhausted the whole next day.

By following these tips I was able to pass all exams on score 8 or higher out of 10. Also, I had 1-2 hours free time each day and was able to participate in some olympiads/hackathons each weekends.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"they published,","labels":["Grammar"],"start":133,"end":148},{"text":"at the last day.","labels":["Grammar"],"start":153,"end":169},{"text":"For example you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":170,"end":185},{"text":"will","labels":["Grammar"],"start":212,"end":216},{"text":"realize","labels":["Grammar"],"start":255,"end":262},{"text":"is ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":271,"end":274},{"text":"need","labels":["Grammar"],"start":289,"end":293},{"text":"earlier you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":344,"end":355},{"text":"because","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":324,"end":331},{"text":"deadline","labels":["Grammar"],"start":382,"end":390},{"text":"plan","labels":["Grammar"],"start":436,"end":440},{"text":"homework, so","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":502,"end":514},{"text":"task","labels":["Grammar"],"start":548,"end":552},{"text":" think, that","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":669,"end":681},{"text":"-- you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":721,"end":727},{"text":"tips I ","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":799,"end":806},{"text":"on score ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":833,"end":842},{"text":"hours free","labels":["Grammar"],"start":881,"end":891},{"text":"weekends","labels":["Grammar"],"start":968,"end":976}],"strengths":[{"text":"For example ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":170,"end":182},{"text":"Also","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":865,"end":869},{"text":"You should know ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":458,"end":474},{"text":"stick to it","labels":["Lexical choice ✓"],"start":445,"end":456}],"comments":[["since"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07085","cefr_level":"B2","task":"Some people think that schools pay too much attention to preparing students for exams. How much do you agree? State your opinion and write 2 arguments to support it.","student_text":"It is important for every student to pass exams but sometimes schools pay to much attention to them. Now I am going to prove my point.
First example is a fact that in high school students are mostly studying topics which are useful for graduating exams. This means that other topics are not so widely studied. And it is really sad because different students would need different material in future and may not even need exam topics.

Another example is the fact, that lots of schools pay too much attention to solving tasks from exams. This means that student have to do similar tasks again and again instead of doing different ones and learning something new. And what is even more insane schools usually give for this process whole 11th grade and sometimes even 10th. And it is not all: in 9th grade there are also exams, which means that nearly 3 last grades are given for exams.

That why I think that schools pay too much attention to preparing students for exams.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"exams but","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":42,"end":51},{"text":"to","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":74,"end":76},{"text":"First example ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":135,"end":149},{"text":"which","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":215,"end":220},{"text":"graduating","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":236,"end":246},{"text":" in future","labels":["Grammar"],"start":387,"end":397},{"text":"Another example","labels":["Grammar"],"start":434,"end":449},{"text":"fact, that","labels":["Grammar"],"start":457,"end":467},{"text":"solving","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":510,"end":517},{"text":"student","labels":["Grammar"],"start":552,"end":559},{"text":"insane ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":683,"end":690},{"text":"give for this process whole 11th","labels":["Lexical choice","Sentence structure"],"start":706,"end":738},{"text":"it","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":774,"end":776},{"text":"n 9th","labels":["Grammar"],"start":790,"end":795},{"text":"That why","labels":["Grammar"],"start":884,"end":892}],"strengths":[{"text":"I am going to prove my point.","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":105,"end":134},{"text":"may not even need exam topics.","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":402,"end":432},{"text":"First example ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":135,"end":149},{"text":"Another example","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":434,"end":449},{"text":" I think ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":892,"end":901}],"comments":[["hedging"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07004","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university!
